claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you tell me **how many children you would like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for your help. I'd like to claim Child Benefit for my child. I have one child - Alex, who is 9 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to meet you, and I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you a few more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex?** This usually means the child lives with you, or you're contributing to the cost of looking after them at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex. Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you!

Now, could you tell me **is Alex in full-time education or training?** And if so, could you let me know what type of education or training they are in (for example, primary school, secondary school, etc.)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is in primary school - just your typical 9-year-old at school during the week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you for confirming that!

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?** This includes England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! Just one more question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any one time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information! Based on what you've told me, I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex is 9 years old and in primary school
- Alex lives with you, so you're responsible for them
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

✅ **Age requirement met** – Alex is 9 years old, which is under the age of 16.

✅ **UK residency** – You live in the UK (Manchester).

✅ **Responsibility for the child** – Alex lives with you, so you meet the responsibility requirement.

✅ **No duplicate claims** – No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

You can apply for Child Benefit online through the GOV.UK website. If you have any other questions about the process, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for Alex as the child lives with them. Alex is in approved education (primary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit: they live in the UK, they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for that child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for Alex as the child lives with them. Alex is in approved education (primary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit: they live in the UK, they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for that child.
